Ack.description: A minimal ok ack for a mutation with no return payload (the `{ ok: true }` shape a delete returns).

ok.description: Always true on success (failures answer a non-200 with `{ error }`).

Ack.title: Ack

AggregatedNutrition.description: A recipe's nutrition rolled up from its items, per 100 g.

caloriesPer100g.description: Calories per 100 g; None when no item carries calorie data.

readings.description: Per-100 g readings summed across items, keyed by nutrient name.

AggregatedNutrition.title: AggregatedNutrition

Amount.description:
A quantity as entered plus its canonical mass: `grams` is the ground
 truth all math runs on; `amount`/`unit` preserve how the user wrote it.

amount.description: Quantity in `unit`, as entered; None when only the mass is known.

unit.description: Display unit (cup, tbsp, g); None when only the mass is known.

Amount.title: Amount

ConversationSummary.description: One conversation row of the DM list, newest-message first.

lastBody.description: Body of the newest message (the list's preview line).

lastIsMine.description: True when the last message is the viewer's own (the list renders "You: …").

unread.description: Count of messages the viewer has not read (SQLite COUNT() is i64).

ConversationSummary.title: ConversationSummary

DayLog.description:
One diary day: the entries plus server-computed nutrient totals, each rolled up from its entry via
 the SAME recursive CTE recipes use (so a logged recipe expands through its nesting). PRIVATE — only
 ever returned to its owner, never listed, never in the anonymous pull.

calories.description: Total calories for the day; None when no entry carries calorie data.

date.description: The 'YYYY-MM-DD' this day covers (echoes the request).

entries.description: The day's live entries, newest logged first.

totals.description: Per-nutrient absolute totals for the day, ordered by nutrient name.

DayLog.title: DayLog

IngredientCard.description: Ingredient browser card (leaf ingredients — those not backing a recipe).

caloriesPer100g.description: Calories per 100 g, when known (list-card badge).

slug.description: Slug for the canonical link; fall back to `/ingredients/<id>`.

username.description:
Owner handle: an OWNED ingredient is canonical at `/<username>/ingredients/<slug>` (browsable
 under its creator); None = the communal catalog, canonical at `/ingredients/<slug>`.

IngredientCard.title: IngredientCard

IngredientEditData.description: IngredientForm edit-mode source data (per-100g; the frontend scales to per-serving).

canEdit.description:
Whether the current viewer owns this ingredient — drives the edit affordance in the UI. Always
 true on the owner-only edit-load path; on the detail path it reflects ownership (false for
 anonymous + non-owner viewers). The real guard stays server-side.

creator.description: Owner handle (None = the communal catalog) — the detail page's breadcrumb + canonical URL.

deleted.description:
Soft-deleted (tombstoned) by its owner: delisted from browse/search but preserved for the
 recipes that use it. Detail renders a badge; lists never surface it.

description.description: Optional description shown on the detail page.

nutrients.description: Per-100 g nutrient rows as stored (the form scales to per-serving).

packageGrams.description: Package mass in grams, when declared (price-per-100 g math).

servingGrams.description: Serving size in grams, when declared.

slug.description: Canonical URL segment `/ingredients/<slug>`. None only pre-backfill.

IngredientEditData.title: IngredientEditData

IngredientSearchResult.description: One ingredient hit in the recipe composer's search box.

readings.description: Per-100 g nutrient readings, for the composer's live nutrition preview.

servingGrams.description:
Serving size in grams, when the ingredient declares one (the composer's
 default line quantity).

IngredientSearchResult.title: IngredientSearchResult

IngredientSlugHit.description: Resolution of an ingredient slug (current or historical) to its row.

canonicalSlug.description:
The ingredient's CURRENT slug — differs when the hit came from slug
 history, in which case the caller 301s here.

ingredientId.description: The ingredient the slug resolves to.

username.description:
Owner handle when the ingredient is user-owned: `/ingredients/<slug>` 301s to
 `/<username>/ingredients/<slug>` (the catalog stays at the global path).

IngredientSlugHit.title: IngredientSlugHit

LogEntryView.description:
One diary entry as read: what was logged, how much, and — when the logged row is itself a
 recipe-as-ingredient — a link to that recipe (mirrors RecipeItem.recipe_id). `calories` is this
 entry's own contribution (its per-100g rollup × grams/100), for the row display.

amount.description: The amount logged (display form + canonical grams).

calories.description: This entry's calorie contribution (per-100g rollup × grams/100), when calorie data exists.

ingredientId.description: The logged ingredient's id (a recipe's as-ingredient id when a recipe was logged).

name.description: Ingredient (or recipe) display name at read time.

recipeId.description: Set when the logged row is itself a recipe-as-ingredient, so the UI links to the recipe page.

slot.description: Meal slot; currently unused by the UI.

LogEntryView.title: LogEntryView

LogPull.description:
The viewer's FULL diary for authed device sync — a separate channel from the anonymous content pull,
 which never carries private log data. The desktop reconciles this into its local cache via
 `apply_log_pull` (its OWN reconciliation, isolated from `apply_pull`'s content rebuild).

entries.description: Every live entry the viewer owns, newest logged first.

LogPull.title: LogPull

LogPullEntry.description:
One diary entry in the authed pull: everything to rebuild it on a replica VERBATIM — its frozen
 nutrition snapshot (calories + per-nutrient rows) and its display amount. The snapshot is NOT
 recomputed on apply; the server's is authoritative.

ingredientId.description: The logged ingredient (or a recipe's as-ingredient id).

unit.description: Display unit the user picked, if any.

LogPullEntry.title: LogPullEntry

LogPullNutrient.description: One nutrient of a pulled entry's FROZEN snapshot (per 100 g) — applied verbatim, never recomputed.
LogPullNutrient.title: LogPullNutrient

Message.description: One DM as the thread screen renders it.

mine.description: True when the viewer sent it (clients render alignment off this, not off raw ids).

Message.title: Message

kind.description:
Notification kind tag (e.g. "ingredient-updated"); selects the
 payload shape and the client-side renderer.

payload.description:
Parsed payload — per-kind (kind "ingredient-updated": `{ingredient: {id,name,slug}, by: {name,username}}`),
 exported as specta's own `serde_json::Value` shape.

read.description: Whether the viewer has opened it.

NutrientTotal.description:
One nutrient's ABSOLUTE total for a day (summed across entries), keyed by name + unit. Distinct
 from `Reading`, which is per-100g.

amount.description: Absolute amount for the day, in `unit`.

NutrientTotal.title: NutrientTotal

Party.description: The other party, as the conversation list + thread header shows them.
Party.title: Party

PostFull.description: Full post — `body` is the parsed JSON block list the web renders.

body.description: The parsed JSON block list, exported as specta's own `serde_json::Value` shape.

PostFull.title: PostFull

description.description: One-to-two sentence summary (index cards + meta description).

Profile.description:
A public profile: the handle, the display name, and the user's visible recipes. Shared by the
 server's `/api/content/profile` endpoint and the desktop DAL so both render the identical screen.

avatarKey.description: Media key of the profile avatar; clients compose `<api base>/<key>`.

ingredients.description:
The user's LEAF ingredients (created or imported by them), visible to the viewer and not
 tombstoned — browsable under `/<username>/ingredients/<slug>`.

recipes.description: The user's recipes visible to the viewer, as cards.

Profile.title: Profile

PullIngredient.description: One ingredient row as pulled (server-authoritative mirror source).

deletedAt.description:
Soft-delete tombstone (ms epoch). Tombstoned rows STAY in the pull — recipes that use them
 need the data — and clients mirror the flag so their local list/search filtering matches.

packageGrams.description: Package mass in grams, when declared.

price.description:
Cents (USD). i32 END-TO-END: SaveIngredientInput (the only write path) is i32, the desktop
 mirror is i32, the TS side is number — an i64 here was silent width drift in the wire contract
 (found by the specta-side repo audit; the class of bug a generated client would make impossible).
 Cents (USD). i32 END-TO-END: SaveIngredientInput (the only write path) is i32, the desktop
 mirror is i32, the TS side is number — an i64 here was silent width drift in the wire
 contract (found by the specta-side repo audit; the class of bug a generated client kills).

servingGrams.description: Serving size in grams, when declared.

slug.description: Current slug; mirrored verbatim so local links match the server.

userId.description: Owner id; None = the communal catalog.

visibility.description: Visibility as stored — the shared enum, not a loose string (same wire bytes).

PullIngredient.title: PullIngredient

unit.description: Display unit the author picked; None = grams.

PullPayload.description:
One full sync pull: every server row visible to the device's user.
 Server-authoritative — the desktop mirrors these verbatim.

ingredients.description: All visible ingredients, with their nutrient rows.

recipes.description: All visible recipes, with their lines.

users.description:
The creators of the rows above — public identity only. The desktop mirrors these into its
 local `users` cache so creator handles and `/<username>` profiles resolve on-device, logged
 out included. Users without a username are omitted (their content renders creatorless,
 exactly as the server serves it).

PullPayload.title: PullPayload

PullReading.description: One nutrient reading as pulled, per 100 g.

unit.description: Unit for the quantity (g, mg, µg).

PullReading.title: PullReading

PullRecipe.description: One recipe row as pulled (server-authoritative mirror source).

asIngredientId.description:
The recipe's as-ingredient pair id — must survive the mirror so
 consuming items' FKs stay intact.

batchGrams.description: Total batch mass in grams, when declared.

servingGrams.description: Serving size in grams, when declared.

slug.description: Current slug; mirrored verbatim so local links match the server.

userId.description: Owner id; None = ownerless seed content.

visibility.description:
Visibility as stored — the shared enum, not a loose string (same wire bytes; the
 TS side tightens to the "public" | "private" | "unlisted" union).

PullRecipe.title: PullRecipe

PullUser.description: One creator as pulled — the public identity surface for content in this payload.

avatarKey.description: Media key of the profile avatar; clients compose `<api base>/<key>`.

PullUser.title: PullUser

Reading.description: One nutrient measurement, normalized per 100 g.

amountPer100g.description: Quantity per 100 g of the food.

unit.description: Display unit for the quantity (g, mg, µg).

Reading.title: Reading

RecentIngredient.description:
A recently/frequently logged ingredient, surfaced so re-logging is fast (the add-flow prepends
 these before global search — personal ranking beats global relevance for logging speed).

count.description: How many times the user has logged it (lifetime, non-deleted).

ingredientId.description: The ingredient's id (a recipe's as-ingredient id when a recipe).

lastGrams.description: The most recent log's grams, to prefill the amount on re-log.

lastLoggedAt.description: When it was last logged (ms epoch).

lastUnit.description: The most recent log's display unit, if any.

recipeId.description: Set when it's a recipe-as-ingredient (the UI can badge/link it).

RecentIngredient.title: RecentIngredient

RecipeCard.description: Recipe list/browse card — the light projection for grids.

photoKey.description:
Media key of the hero photo (attached to the recipe's as-ingredient); clients compose the
 URL as `<api base>/<key>`. None = no photo yet (cards render the placeholder tile).

slug.description: The recipe's slug half of the canonical link (see `username`).

subtitle.description: Optional subtitle, shown under the title on cards.

username.description:
Owner handle + slug for the canonical `/<username>/<slug>` link. Optional (pre-backfill /
 ownerless rows); the UI falls back to `/recipes/<id>` when either is missing.

RecipeCard.title: RecipeCard

RecipeEditData.description: RecipeForm edit-mode source data (per-100 g; the frontend scales).

items.description: The recipe's lines in edit form.

servings.description: Declared servings per batch, when set.

RecipeEditData.title: RecipeEditData

RecipeEditItem.description: RecipeForm edit-mode defaults: per-item nutrition included so each row shows live nutrition.

caloriesPer100g.description: Calories per 100 g, when known — the edit screen's live math.

readings.description: Per-100 g readings for the edit screen's live nutrition roll-up.

RecipeEditItem.title: RecipeEditItem

RecipeItem.description: One recipe line as read: the ingredient, its display name, its amount.

amount.description: The line's quantity (display form + canonical grams).

deleted.description:
True only in the DELETER's own recipes: the ingredient is soft-deleted (tombstoned) AND its
 owner owns this recipe. Other users' recipes render the same ingredient normally — a soft
 delete disowns from the catalog without breaking anyone's recipe. Drives the greyed row +
 the "restore?" affordance.

name.description: Ingredient display name at read time.

recipeId.description:
Set when this item is itself a recipe-as-ingredient (e.g. a Biga in a Dough),
 so the UI links to that recipe's page instead of a (sparse) ingredient page.

RecipeItem.title: RecipeItem

RecipeSlugHit.description: Resolution of a recipe slug (current or historical) to its row.

canonicalSlug.description:
The recipe's CURRENT slug. When it differs from the requested slug, the caller 301s to
 `/<username>/<canonical_slug>`.

recipeId.description: The recipe the slug resolves to.

RecipeSlugHit.title: RecipeSlugHit

RecipeView.description: Full recipe detail as rendered by the recipe page.

batchGrams.description:
Total batch mass in grams, when declared — the denominator that
 turns summed item nutrition into per-100 g.

canEdit.description:
Whether the current viewer owns this recipe — drives the edit affordance in the UI. The real
 guard stays server-side (owner-only edit-load + mutation); false for anonymous + non-owner viewers.

creator.description: The owner's username — also the first segment of the canonical URL `/<creator>/<slug>`.

directions.description: Free-text directions (markdown-ish plain text); None = none written.

nutrition.description: Nutrition rolled up from the items, per 100 g.

photoKey.description: Media key of the hero photo — see [`RecipeCard::photo_key`].

serving.description: Serving size, when declared (drives per-serving nutrition).

slug.description: This recipe's current slug (the `/<creator>/<slug>` segment). None only pre-backfill.

RecipeView.title: RecipeView

date.description: User-local calendar date 'YYYY-MM-DD', chosen client-side (no server timezone modeling).

id.description:
Existing entry to update, or None to create (id minted). Client ids are honored so replays
 stay idempotent cross-replica.

ingredientId.description: The ingredient — or a recipe's as_ingredient_id — being logged.

loggedAt.description: When it was logged (ms epoch); None ⇒ now. Set by a sync replay to preserve ordering.

slot.description:
Meal slot (breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack); currently ignored by the UI. Serde-default so
 slot-less payloads deserialize.

unit.description: Display unit the user picked; None = grams. Mirrors RecipeItemInput.

SaveLogEntryInput.title: SaveLogEntryInput

SavedId.description: A create/update ack carrying the affected row's id (the `{ id }` shape the content writes return).

id.description: The created or updated row's id.

SavedId.title: SavedId

SendMessageBody.description:
A DM send, as the client must state it. The server's own deserializer stays lenient (missing
 fields answer 400 with a message rather than a deserialization error); the CONTRACT is that
 both fields are required.
SendMessageBody.title: SendMessageBody

SitemapData.description:
The public, canonical, indexable URLs — everything with a slug that anyone can read. Recipes and
 OWNED ingredients carry their owner handle; unowned ingredients are the catalog namespace.
SitemapData.title: SitemapData

SitemapIngredient.description: One indexable ingredient URL for the sitemap.

username.description:
Owner handle: owned rows are canonical at `/<username>/ingredients/<slug>`; None = catalog
 (`/ingredients/<slug>`).

SitemapIngredient.title: SitemapIngredient

SitemapRecipe.description: Owner handle (first segment of the canonical URL).

username.description: Owner handle (first segment of the canonical URL).

SitemapRecipe.title: SitemapRecipe

Sort.description:
Recipe browser cards. isListed: public catalog + your own (any visibility). `user_id = NULL` never
 matches, so a signed-out viewer (viewer = None) sees only public.
 Public recipe catalog for `viewer` (public rows + the viewer's own), NEWEST FIRST by id — ids are
 ULIDs, so id order is creation order. Keyset-paginated for infinite scroll: pass the last card's `id`
 as `cursor` to get the page after it; `limit` caps the page (None = no limit, i.e. the full list).
 Sort order for the catalog list reads. Recency sorts key on the id (ids are ULIDs, so id order is
 creation order); name sorts use a composite (name, id) keyset since names are not unique. Default
 = Newest (the catalog's first impression).
Sort.title: Sort

Thread.description:
A thread as the thread screen consumes it: the other party (resolved even before any message
 exists, so a profile's "Message" button lands on an empty composer) + the messages, oldest first.

with.description: The other party (resolved even for an empty thread).

Thread.title: Thread

UploadTicket.description: An approved upload: PUT the bytes to `url` (presigned, short-lived), then attach `key`.

key.description: Storage key to attach to the row once the PUT succeeds.

url.description: Presigned, short-lived PUT URL for the bytes.

UploadTicket.title: UploadTicket

User.description:
The signed-in user — the `{id, name, username, email}` the auth routes return, and the viewer the content
 gates scope to. Serializes with bare field names (matching the web's response).

email.description: Login/notification address; visible only to the account itself.

email_verified.description:
Whether `users.email_verified_at` is set — surfaced to the clients so they can prompt for
 verification (and gate verified-only actions later) without a second round trip.

username.description: Public handle backing `/<username>`. Assigned at signup (see [`derive_unique_username`]).

User.title: User

Visibility.description:
The app is public-default sharing: `public` = anyone lists+reads; `unlisted` = readable by
 direct id/link but not listed; `private` = owner only. Stored on `ingredients` — a recipe IS an
 ingredient (`as_ingredient_id`), so this one field covers both. Ownership (`user_id`) gates
 EDITING, not reading.
Visibility.title: Visibility

x-specta-enum-descriptions.unlisted: Readable via direct link; never listed or searched.

info.description: The typed surface of api.vegify.app: the public read endpoints, the content pull, messages, notifications, and the session probe. Auth flows and write acks still answer ad-hoc JSON and join this document as their shapes graduate into vegify-api-types. Endpoints marked 'Requires bearer' expect an Authorization: Bearer <token> header.
info.title: vegify.app HTTP API

get.description: Requires bearer. The signed-in user, or 401.
Public. Card shapes only, no bodies.
Public. Null when the slug matches nothing.

200.description: The post with its block-list body, or null

get.description: Public. Null → 404; a canonicalSlug differing from the request → 301.
Public (visibility-gated). Null when absent or not visible.
Requires bearer; owner-gated. Null when absent or not editable.
Public. Same paging contract as /api/content/recipes.
Public; optional bearer lets owners see their own non-public shelves.
Public; optional bearer widens the payload to the viewer's own non-public rows.

200.description: Every visible row, in mutation shape

get.description: Public. Null → 404; a canonicalSlug differing from the request → 301.
Public (visibility-gated). Null when absent or not visible to the viewer.
Requires bearer; owner-gated. Null when absent or not editable.
Public. Sort + keyset cursor; viewer's own non-public rows included when authed.
Public. The recipe composer's search box.
Public. The web shell renders sitemap.xml from this.

200.description: Every public canonical URL's source row

get.description: Requires bearer; PRIVATE to the viewer. The date's entries plus server-computed nutrient totals (each entry rolled up through the same nested-recipe CTE recipes use).

delete.description: Requires bearer; owner-gated. Soft-deletes by id (the row survives).

patch.description: Requires bearer; owner-gated. Same body as POST, with the entry id set.

post.description: Requires bearer; PRIVATE to the viewer. Logs `grams` of an ingredient (or a recipe via its as-ingredient id) against a client-chosen calendar date. Upsert-by-id: a body id updates that entry (owner-gated), else a new one is minted.

get.description: Requires bearer; PRIVATE to the viewer. The viewer's ENTIRE diary, each entry with its frozen snapshot — the desktop's local-first cache sync channel, never the anonymous content pull.

200.description: Every live diary entry with its frozen snapshot

get.description: Requires bearer; PRIVATE to the viewer. Distinct ingredients the viewer has logged, newest first, to prepend the add-flow's search.
Requires bearer. Newest-message first.
Requires bearer. Oldest message first; resolves the party even when empty.

200.description: The thread with the named party

get.description: Requires bearer. Per-kind payload parsed inline.
Plain-text ok; the deploy gate and uptime checks poll this.
